Wait, but the initial configuration had the light green square at center [40:41, 10:11]. After clicking on the red shape, it moved to bottom-left [42:43, 8:9]. So the current configuration is almost the same as the initial one, except the light green square is in a different position.
Let me think about what the goal might be. The right shapes have a "stepped" pattern. The colored squares move within the gray boxes. The left blocks rearrange when I click on the right shapes.

Let me try yet another approach. Maybe the game is about matching the left blocks to the right shapes, and the level completes when the left blocks form the same pattern as the right shapes. - Blue shape: 6 rows, 15 cols. Same pattern.
The left blocks need to form a similar pattern. The blocks are:

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking on the blue shape to swap blue rect and checkerboard
# This would put checkerboard at top and blue rect at bottom
predict(expect_change=True, note='Click on blue shape to swap blue rect and checkerboard')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 35, 'col': 48}])

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("=== Left blocks after clicking blue ===")
for r in range(18, 44):
    line = ascii_lines[r][7:22]
    if any(c not in 'c' for c in line):
        print(f"  {r}: {line}")

print(f"\n  Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

# Check if this matches the initial state
init_frame = history[0].frame
init_ascii = init_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Initial left blocks ===")
for r in range(18, 44):
    line = init_ascii[r][7:22]
    if any(c not in 'c' for c in line):
        print(f"  {r}: {line}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
